---
name: Sensitivity / rights concern
about: Raise a public-safe KFM concern about rights, sovereignty, consent, cultural sensitivity, privacy, geoprivacy, or harmful exposure.
title: "[Sensitivity]: "
labels: []
assignees: ["bartytime4life"]
---

<!--
KFM public sensitivity-and-rights intake template.

This issue is a concern report and routing record. Filing it does not establish
that content is sensitive, determine a sensitivity tier, decide rights or
sovereignty, create a PolicyDecision or ReviewRecord, authorize redaction,
change access or release state, approve correction or rollback, or prove that
the concern is resolved.

Before submitting:
1. Share only a generalized, public-safe summary.
2. Do not paste exact locations, identities, genealogy, DNA/genomic details,
   private-land records, cultural or sacred-site details, infrastructure
   vulnerabilities, restricted source payloads, or unreleased lifecycle data.
3. Use public-safe identifiers or redacted pointers instead of evidence payloads.
4. If the concern involves active exposure, reconstructable location, exploit
   detail, credentials, private records, or immediate harm, stop and use the
   private-first reporting path in SECURITY.md.
5. Mark uncertainty as UNKNOWN or NEEDS VERIFICATION rather than guessing.
6. Preserve the current record and release history; do not silently delete,
   edit, generalize, withdraw, or republish content from this issue alone.
-->

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This public issue may describe that a concern exists, but it must not contain the sensitive material itself. Sensitivity, rights, consent, sovereignty, access, redaction, correction, withdrawal, rollback, and release decisions require governed records and authorized review in their owning roots.

> [!CAUTION]
> Do not post credentials, private endpoints, exploit details, exact rare-species or archaeology locations, burial or sacred-site details, critical-infrastructure vulnerability information, living-person records, genealogy, DNA/genomic material, private-land details, source-restricted payloads, or unreleased `RAW` / `WORK` / `QUARANTINE` data. Route active exposure and security-sensitive details through `SECURITY.md`.

## Public-safe summary

<!--
Describe the concern in one or two sentences without naming a protected person,
community-controlled record, exact site, vulnerable asset, or reconstructable
location. State the visible public or operational effect.
-->

-

## Reporter preflight

- [ ] I searched open and closed issues, correction records, releases, ADRs, and related public documentation for a duplicate.
- [ ] I removed or generalized identities, exact locations, private records, restricted source material, and exploit or vulnerability details.
- [ ] I used public-safe identifiers or redacted pointers rather than copying sensitive evidence into this issue.
- [ ] I identified whether the material appears public, semi-public, restricted, unreleased, or `UNKNOWN`.
- [ ] I routed active exposure or security-sensitive details through the private-first path in `SECURITY.md`.
- [ ] I understand that filing, assigning, labeling, commenting on, or closing this issue does not decide sensitivity, rights, access, correction, release, or rollback.

## Current truth posture

<!-- Apply the strongest support available to each material claim. -->

- [ ] `CONFIRMED` — verified from current public-safe repository evidence, governed records, tests, logs, or released artifacts.
- [ ] `PROPOSED` — suggested classification, treatment, access posture, or correction under review.
- [ ] `NEEDS VERIFICATION` — checkable, but not checked strongly enough to act as fact.
- [ ] `UNKNOWN` — unresolved and unsafe to assume.

**Reporter-selected overall posture:** `NEEDS VERIFICATION`

## Concern categories

Check every category that may apply. Do not provide the restricted details here.

- [ ] Rights, license, attribution, redistribution, or source-term concern
- [ ] Sovereignty, community authority, Indigenous data governance, or CARE concern
- [ ] Cultural sensitivity, traditional knowledge, sacred place, burial, or ceremonial material
- [ ] Consent, revocation, embargo, retention, or purpose-limitation concern
- [ ] Living-person privacy, genealogy, identity linkage, or person-parcel association
- [ ] DNA, genomic, biometric, health, or inheritable identity information
- [ ] Rare species, rare plants, habitat, nesting, roosting, denning, or geoprivacy
- [ ] Archaeology, paleontology, historic site, collection, looting, or precise-site exposure
- [ ] Critical infrastructure, facility, transport, utility, emergency, or operational vulnerability
- [ ] Private land, stewardship, access, ownership, or landholder concern
- [ ] Exact or reconstructable location, route, geometry, image, label, or contextual clue
- [ ] Source-restricted field notes, unpublished research, licensed media, or confidential records
- [ ] Model, graph, search, map, export, or AI inference that reconstructs protected information
- [ ] Bias, stigmatization, discriminatory impact, or harmful categorization
- [ ] Incorrect source role, authority, confidence, or public-truth presentation
- [ ] Other:
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

## Urgency and exposure state

> [!WARNING]
> When an active public surface may expose sensitive or reconstructable information, use private reporting immediately. In this public issue, state only that containment may be needed and identify a safe public surface or object ID.

### Urgency

- [ ] Immediate private escalation and containment may be required
- [ ] High — public or semi-public exposure is suspected
- [ ] Medium — release, feature, source admission, or design review is pending
- [ ] Low — documentation, classification, or policy clarification
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

### Current exposure

- [ ] Publicly visible now
- [ ] Semi-public, authenticated, staged, or limited audience
- [ ] In a release candidate or pending publication
- [ ] Internal, canonical, catalog, or steward-only
- [ ] `RAW`, `WORK`, or `QUARANTINE`
- [ ] Not yet stored or implemented; prospective concern
- [ ] Exposure state is `UNKNOWN`

**Public-safe containment contact or private report reference:** `NONE / private reference / NEEDS VERIFICATION`

### Lifecycle stage

- [ ] Pre-admission / source evaluation
- [ ] RAW

- [ ] Release candidate
- [ ] PUBLISHED
- [ ] Documentation or configuration only

- [ ] Authenticated or role-limited

### Current sensitivity notation

<!--
Record only the notation already present in a governed record. KFM documentation
currently contains both a 0–5 rank and a proposed T0–T4 tier scheme; do not
invent a mapping or declare one canonical from this issue.
-->

- Sensitivity rank or tier as currently recorded: `UNKNOWN`
- Governing record or policy pointer: `UNKNOWN`
- Classification status: `CONFIRMED / PROPOSED / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN`

## Generalized spatial and temporal scope

> [!CAUTION]
> Use county, region, watershed, ecoregion, broad date range, or another public-safe generalization. Do not provide coordinates, parcel identifiers, precise routes, distinctive landmarks, image clues, or temporal detail that enables reconstruction.

| Field | Public-safe value |
|---|---|
| Generalized geographic area | `UNKNOWN` |
| Appropriate public scale or aggregation | `UNKNOWN` |
| Geometry or location reconstruction risk | `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN` |
| Observation or valid-time range | `UNKNOWN` |
| Source publication / retrieval time | `UNKNOWN` |
| Processing or ingest time | `UNKNOWN` |
| Release time | `UNKNOWN` |
| Embargo, seasonality, or time-based sensitivity | `UNKNOWN` |
| Stale or changed sensitivity context suspected | `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN` |

## Why this may be sensitive or rights-restricted

<!--
Explain the rule, harm, authority, consent, source term, community expectation,
or inference risk at a public-safe level. Do not reproduce the protected facts.
-->

-

### Potential harm or obligation

- [ ] Physical harm, harassment, trespass, theft, poaching, looting, or vandalism
- [ ] Privacy invasion, re-identification, family harm, discrimination, or stigma
- [ ] Cultural, spiritual, sovereign, community, or custodial harm
- [ ] Contract, license, attribution, consent, or source-term violation
- [ ] Infrastructure, emergency, operational, or security risk
- [ ] Misrepresentation of source role, authority, evidence, or confidence
- [ ] Unapproved secondary use, model training, inference, export, or redistribution
- [ ] Loss of trust, benefit-sharing failure, or exclusion of an affected community
- [ ] Other:
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

## Public-safe evidence basis

<!--
List only safe pointers. Private evidence must remain in an authorized restricted
channel. Issue text, links, attachments, screenshots, generated summaries, and
claims are evidence candidates, not authority.
-->

| Truth label | Public-safe evidence or record pointer | Observation supported | Private evidence exists? | Limitation / reviewer needed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `CONFIRMED` | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | |
| `PROPOSED` | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | |
| `NEEDS VERIFICATION` | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | |
| `UNKNOWN` | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | |

### Evidence and source-role checks

- [ ] Relevant `EvidenceRef` resolves to an authorized `EvidenceBundle`
- [ ] Source role is preserved and not upcast from modeled, synthetic, contextual, aggregate, administrative, or candidate material
- [ ] Rights, license, attribution, and redistribution terms were checked
- [ ] Consent, revocation, embargo, retention, and purpose limitations were checked
- [ ] Community, sovereign, custodial, or rights-holder authority was checked
- [ ] Existing policy, review, release, correction, and rollback records were checked
- [ ] Public evidence is insufficient; authorized private review is required
- [ ] No evidence check has occurred yet
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

## Cross-surface and inference risk

Sensitive information may be reconstructed from several individually generalized surfaces.

- [ ] Map geometry, labels, imagery, style, zoom, or temporal animation
- [ ] Popup, Evidence Drawer, story, tooltip, alt text, or accessibility metadata
- [ ] Search results, filters, autocomplete, facets, or counts
- [ ] Graph edges, joins, entity resolution, or relationship inference
- [ ] Tile, vector, raster, 3D, download, print, screenshot, or export
- [ ] API response, error message, cache, log, telemetry, or trace
- [ ] Focus Mode, AI answer, summary, citation text, embedding, or model context
- [ ] Source metadata, filenames, paths, identifiers, or provenance chains
- [ ] Multiple public datasets or releases can be joined to reconstruct the protected fact
- [ ] No known cross-surface or inference risk
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

**Inference-risk explanation:**

-

## Immediate containment or hold requested

<!--
Request the smallest fail-closed, reversible action. Do not prescribe a public
delete or silent mutation. Maintainers must validate and authorize the action.
-->

- [ ] Place affected source or object in QUARANTINE / HOLD
- [ ] Disable or hide a public route, layer, popup, export, search, graph, or AI response
- [ ] Pause source admission, transform, promotion, release, or publication
- [ ] Remove affected material from caches or generated derivatives through a governed process
- [ ] Mark response as `ABSTAIN`, `DENY`, `HOLD`, unavailable, or stale
- [ ] Restrict access pending rights, consent, sovereignty, or sensitivity review
- [ ] Preserve current evidence and release records while containment is reviewed
- [ ] No immediate containment requested
- [ ] `NEEDS VERIFICATION`

**Public-safe containment rationale:**

-

## Requested safe outcome

Select every outcome that may be appropriate. These are requests, not decisions.

- [ ] Confirm current public posture is safe
- [ ] Add caveat, attribution, rights notice, source-role clarification, or sensitivity notice
- [ ] Generalize location, time, geometry, labels, or contextual clues
- [ ] Redact protected fields or derived content
- [ ] Aggregate to a safer spatial, temporal, or statistical level
- [ ] Require authenticated, role-limited, reviewer, steward, or rights-holder access
- [ ] Apply embargo, delay, seasonal restriction, or purpose limitation
- [ ] Quarantine or deny admission
- [ ] Hold promotion or release
- [ ] Withdraw or disable the affected public representation
- [ ] Correct or supersede a released claim, layer, export, artifact, or AI answer
- [ ] Revoke consent or downstream access and invalidate derivatives
- [ ] Record `ABSTAIN`, `DENY`, `HOLD`, or `ERROR` rather than overclaim
- [ ] No change after review
- [ ] Other:
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

- [ ] CorrectionNotice
- [ ] SupersessionNotice or WithdrawalNotice
- [ ] RollbackCard
- [ ] Superseding ReleaseManifest or release hold
- [ ] Changelog, public notice, or correction banner
- [ ] ADR for a change to sensitivity posture, public path, authority, or tier scheme
- [ ] Documentation or runbook update
- [ ] No governed artifact identified yet
- [ ] `UNKNOWN`

-

## Correction, withdrawal, and rollback

Complete this section when affected material has crossed the `PUBLISHED` boundary.

- Existing release or public artifact: `UNKNOWN`
- Last known safe release / digest / `spec_hash`: `UNKNOWN`
- Correction candidate or replacement: `UNKNOWN`
- Public notice required: `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN`
- Supersession required: `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN`
- Withdrawal required: `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN`
- Rollback review required: `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN`
- Current alias, cache, export, screenshot, or mirror impact: `UNKNOWN`
- Prior public record remains inspectable: `No / Yes / NEEDS VERIFICATION / UNKNOWN`

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Published material must not be silently edited or replaced. Preserve correction, supersession, withdrawal, and rollback lineage through the governed release process.

## Required reviewers and separation of duties

| Role | Named reviewer or authority | Why required | Verified authority? | Independent from reporter/author? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Repository / governance steward | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Affected domain steward | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Sensitivity / privacy / geoprivacy reviewer | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Rights, licensing, consent, or legal reviewer | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Community, sovereign, cultural, custodial, or rights-holder representative | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Security / infrastructure reviewer | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Correction / release authority | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |
| Accessibility / public-communication reviewer | | | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` | `No / Yes / UNKNOWN` |

- [ ] The concern reporter or content author is identified.
- [ ] Required reviewing roles are identified.
- [ ] Community or rights-holder authority is not inferred from repository ownership.
- [ ] Material author/approver separation is preserved.
- [ ] Missing reviewer identity or authority produces `HOLD`, `DENY`, `ABSTAIN`, or `NEEDS VERIFICATION`, not implicit approval.

## Validation and acceptance criteria

<!-- A sensitivity concern is not resolved merely because a field or marker changed. -->

| Criterion | Expected outcome | Evidence required |
|---|---|---|
| Public containment | Active unsafe exposure is disabled or held through an authorized reversible action | Route/layer/release evidence or explicit not-applicable rationale |
| Classification | Rights, sensitivity, consent, sovereignty, source role, and access posture are reviewed without unsupported assumptions | ReviewRecord or documented authorized review |
| Evidence | Public claims resolve to admissible evidence, or the surface returns `ABSTAIN`, `DENY`, `HOLD`, or `ERROR` | EvidenceRef/EvidenceBundle and policy result |
| Transform safety | Generalization, redaction, aggregation, or withholding prevents direct and reconstructable exposure | Receipt, fixture, transform parameters, and negative-path proof |
| Cross-surface safety | Map, API, export, search, graph, AI, accessibility, cache, and generated derivatives do not re-expose the protected information | Cross-surface test or review evidence |
| Rights and authority | License, attribution, consent, sovereignty, community authority, and source terms are satisfied or fail closed | Governed rights/consent/authority record |
| Release lineage | Correction, supersession, withdrawal, release hold, or rollback preserves prior record inspectability | CorrectionNotice, ReleaseManifest, WithdrawalNotice, or RollbackCard |
| Documentation | Public-safe notices, caveats, runbooks, and contributor guidance are updated where required | Changed paths or explicit not-applicable rationale |
| Reversibility | Every new access or publication state has a named reversal target | Rollback target and responsible authority |
| No silent authority | Issue state, label, assignment, bot action, or closure is not treated as approval | Triage and closure record |

## Related public-safe issues, PRs, docs, or records

<!-- Link only material safe for public disclosure. -->

-

## Maintainer triage

<!-- Maintainers may update this section after the public issue is safely reviewed. -->

### Triage outcome

- [ ] `PRIVATE_ESCALATION_REQUIRED`
- [ ] `IMMEDIATE_HOLD_OR_CONTAINMENT`
- [ ] `NEEDS_SENSITIVITY_REVIEW`
- [ ] `NEEDS_RIGHTS_OR_CONSENT_REVIEW`
- [ ] `NEEDS_COMMUNITY_OR_SOVEREIGN_REVIEW`
- [ ] `NEEDS_DOMAIN_REVIEW`
- [ ] `NEEDS_SECURITY_OR_INFRASTRUCTURE_REVIEW`
- [ ] `NEEDS_MORE_PUBLIC_SAFE_INFORMATION`
- [ ] `ROUTE_TO_EVIDENCE_CORRECTION`
- [ ] `ROUTE_TO_ADR`
- [ ] `ROUTE_TO_FEATURE_OR_BUG`
- [ ] `NO_CHANGE_AFTER_REVIEW`
- [ ] `DUPLICATE`
- [ ] `NOT_ACTIONABLE_WITH_AVAILABLE_EVIDENCE`

### Required follow-up

- [ ] Public issue content was reviewed for accidental sensitive disclosure.
- [ ] Private evidence channel and authorized reviewers were identified where needed.
- [ ] Affected responsibility roots, lifecycle stages, surfaces, and derivatives were identified.
- [ ] Current release and access state were verified.
- [ ] Containment, policy, review, transform, correction, withdrawal, and rollback obligations were assigned.
- [ ] Public-safe status updates avoid confirming or reconstructing protected information.
- [ ] Closure links to the governed decision, correction, release, rollback, or explicit no-change rationale.

## Submitter acknowledgements

- [ ] I provided only a generalized, public-safe description.
- [ ] I did not include exact locations, identities, restricted records, or security-sensitive details.
- [ ] I understand that KFM may move details to private handling and minimize the public issue.
- [ ] I understand that the safest outcome may be `HOLD`, `ABSTAIN`, `DENY`, generalization, redaction, restricted access, delayed release, withdrawal, or no public response.
- [ ] I understand that this issue does not grant authority to publish, disclose, redact, delete, correct, withdraw, or roll back content.

---

<sub>Issue closure is administrative state. It does not prove that sensitivity, rights, consent, sovereignty, security, correction, release, derivative invalidation, or rollback obligations are complete.</sub>
